commit.message:
types: re-record range bound spans with inferred element type (#634)

For-loop range literals like `0..10` are synthesized with a fresh TypeVar
for the element type so the loop body can constrain it (e.g. from an
`i32` accumulator).  The *range* TypeVar was already unified correctly,
but the *bound literal spans* (the `0` and `10`) were permanently
recorded as `I64` by the initial `synthesize` call.

Because codegen uses `resolvedTypeOf(span)` in `generateLiteral` to
choose the MLIR constant width, this caused two issues:
1. Bounds were always emitted as i64 constants even when the range was
   inferred to be `Range<i32>`, producing unnecessarily wide IR.
2. A bound that doesn't fit in the inferred type (e.g. `0..300` with
   `i8` constraint) would silently generate a truncated constant —
   e.g. 300 → 44 — causing wrong iteration counts at runtime.

Fix:
- Add `deferred_range_bounds: Vec<(Span, TypeVar, Option<i64>)>` to
  Checker, populated in `check_binary_op` when both bounds are coercible
  integer literals.
- Add `apply_deferred_range_bound_types` which runs after
  `default_unconstrained_range_types` in `check_program`.  It resolves
  each `TypeVar` to its concrete type, validates the literal fits (emitting
  an error if not), and re-records the bound span with the correct type.
- Add a `check_against` arm for `(Expr::Binary{Range|RangeInclusive},
  Range<T>)` so that range literals in typed positions (function args,
  `let r: Range<i32> = 0..10`) check the bounds directly against `T`.

Tests:
- `for_range_infers_i32_from_body_usage`: verifies bound spans are
  re-recorded as I32 (not I64) when body constrains the induction variable.
- `range_literal_check_against_range_i32`: range literal coerces to a
  `Range<i32>` parameter without errors.
- `for_range_unconstrained_defaults_to_i64`: unconstrained range still
  defaults to i64 (no regression).
- `for_range_bound_overflow_errors`: `0..300` with an i8 constraint now
  correctly errors instead of silently truncating.
- `range_literal_assigned_to_range_i32`: `let r: Range<i32> = 0..10`.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(types): enforce actor boundary Send (#609)

* fix(types): enforce actor boundary Send

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(types): Actor<T> handle is always Send/Sync at spawn-capture boundary

The Send check for spawned lambda actor captures did not recognise the
built-in Actor<T> type (returned by 'spawn (x: T) => { ... }') as Send.
Only user-defined actors in self.actors were tested; Actor<T> fell
through to the field-based check and was incorrectly rejected.

This broke the pipeline pattern where one lambda actor captures another:

    let printer = spawn (x: int) => { println(x); };
    let doubler = spawn (x: int) => { printer.send(x * 2); };
                                     ^^^^^^^ Actor<int> capture

Add an explicit arm alongside ActorRef that marks Actor<T> as
Send + Sync + Clone + Debug. Add a regression test.

commit.message:
fix(hew-cli): pass drop_funcs to serialize_to_msgpack in compile_from_source_checked (#633)

* fix(types): mark implicit regex import used on RegexLiteral synthesis

The CLI injects a synthetic `import std::text::regex` when regex literals
are detected in source.  The type checker stores the import's short name
(`regex`) in `import_spans` but, when synthesising the `regex.Pattern`
type for a `RegexLiteral` expression, never inserted `regex` into
`used_modules`.  This caused the end-of-program unused-import sweep to
emit a false-positive "unused import: regex" warning for every file that
contained regex literals (e.g. examples/regex_demo.hew).

Fix: in the `Expr::RegexLiteral` arm of `synthesize_inner`, call
`self.used_modules.borrow_mut().insert("regex")` before returning the
`Ty::Named { name: "regex.Pattern" }` type, mirroring what the named-
module-call path already does for other stdlib modules.

Regression test added in `hew-types/tests/e2e_typecheck.rs`: compiles a
minimal program with an explicit `import std::text::regex` and a regex
literal, then asserts no `UnusedImport` warning is present.

* refactor(hew-observe): extract TraceEvent::is_actionable() predicate

The four-way event-type check ("send" | "spawn" | "crash" | "stop") was
duplicated verbatim in both app.rs (trace navigation scroll) and ui.rs
(timeline rendering).  Extract it as a method on TraceEvent in client.rs —
the type that owns the field — so there is a single source of truth.

Zero behaviour change: same four strings, same filter semantics.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(hew-cli): pass drop_funcs to serialize_to_msgpack in compile_from_source_checked

The compile_from_source_checked function (used by hew eval / REPL path)
called serialize_to_msgpack with 6 arguments, omitting drop_funcs between
handle_type_repr and source_path. The function signature requires 7 args:

  serialize_to_msgpack(program, expr_types, handle_types,
                       handle_type_repr, drop_funcs,  ← missing
                       source_path, line_map)

The first call site (compile(), ~line 701) was already correct.
Only compile_from_source_checked (~line 805) was broken.

Fix: thread meta.drop_funcs into the call, restoring drop-function
metadata parity between the two compile paths.

commit.message:
fix(runtime/wasm): restore actor arena lifecycle parity (#617)

* fix(runtime/wasm): install and reset actor arena during WASM activation

The WASM scheduler's activate_actor_wasm did not call set_current_arena
before dispatch or hew_arena_reset after it, leaving a parity gap with
the native scheduler.

Changes:
- Add arena_install / arena_reset shim fns with cfg guards:
  non-WASM calls real crate::arena fns; wasm32 uses no-op stubs (WASM-TODO)
- In activate_actor_wasm:
  - Replace PREV_ARENA=null with PREV_ARENA=arena_install(a.arena) so the
    actor's arena is installed before dispatch and the prior arena is saved
  - Cache actor_arena before the dispatch loop (avoids dangling read if
    terminate_fn frees the actor)
  - After the loop: let _ = arena_install(PREV_ARENA) to restore the
    previous arena (discard return explicit), then arena_reset(actor_arena)
    with a comment explaining unconditional reset is safe on WASM because
    there is no siglongjmp crash path that could issue a competing reset
- reset_globals: clear thread-local current arena for test isolation
- Add four focused tests: install+clear, cursor reset, reentrant restore,
  null-arena baseline

Pre-existing flaky failures (unrelated to this change):
  hew_node::tests::two_node_remote_* — flaky under concurrent test load on main

Residual gaps out of scope: wasm32 arena allocator, terminate_fn,
sys_len, try_send, mailbox-close.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fixup! fix(runtime/wasm): install and reset actor arena during WASM activation

Repair 12 Clippy errors introduced by the arena lifecycle tests (CI job
69951950131, PR #617):

- items_after_statements (6): hoist static ARENA_DURING_DISPATCH,
  capture_arena_dispatch, alloc_in_dispatch, static OUTER_POST_DISPATCH,
  outer_dispatch, and queue_wasm_message_static to the top of their
  respective test function bodies, before any let-statements.

- undocumented_unsafe_blocks (4): add // SAFETY: comments to the p2-alloc
  block in arena_is_reset_after_activation, and to the three bare unsafe
  blocks inside outer_dispatch and queue_wasm_message_static.

- redundant/unnecessary raw-pointer cast (2, same line): remove the
  redundant 'as *mut *mut HewActor' cast in the inner_ptr state
  assignment; &raw mut inner_ptr is already *mut *mut HewActor.

No behavior changes.

commit.message:
fix: re-arm idle timer after wake-from-standby (race condition)

SinkMonitor may fire on_idle while bt_standby is still True during
the wake flow.  _on_sink_idle() returns early because of the standby
guard, so the idle timer never gets re-armed after wake.

After clearing bt_standby in _reroute_to_bt_sink(), check the sink
state and call _on_sink_idle() if the sink is not running.

Verified on sendspin-test VM: wake → idle timer re-armed → standby
triggers again after 2 min.

commit.message:
serialize: fail closed on unresolved type variables (#569)

* feat: fail-closed serializer boundary for Ty::Var vs Ty::Error

Add TypeExprConversionKind enum and a kind() accessor to
TypeExprConversionError so callers can distinguish three cases without
increasing the struct size:

  - UnresolvedVar  Ty::Var escaped type-checking unresolved (compiler bug)
  - ErrorSentinel  Ty::Error forwarded from a failed typecheck (already reported)
  - Unsupported    structurally valid type not representable in TypeExpr

kind() is derived cheaply from the already-stored ty field, so the
struct layout is unchanged and the result_large_err lint stays clean.

In hew-cli/src/compile.rs enrich_program_ast():
  - UnresolvedVar diagnostics from both the enrich pass and the
    expr-type-map pass abort compilation with a hard error; unresolved
    inference variables in codegen output are a compiler bug.
  - ErrorSentinel diagnostics are silently dropped; type-checking already
    emitted the user-facing error and a duplicate warning is just noise.
  - Unsupported diagnostics continue to emit warnings as before.

Export TypeExprConversionKind from hew-serialize/src/lib.rs.

Tests added/updated in hew-serialize/src/enrich.rs:
  - Updated test_ty_to_type_expr_error_returns_explicit_error to assert
    kind() == ErrorSentinel.
  - Updated test_ty_to_type_expr_var_returns_explicit_error to assert
    kind() == UnresolvedVar.
  - Added test_ty_to_type_expr_generator_returns_unsupported_kind.
  - Added test_kind_discriminants_are_distinct.

112 hew-serialize unit tests pass; 142 hew-cli tests pass;
workspace-wide cargo clippy -D warnings passes.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: remove silent Range<Ty::Var> coercion to bypass fail-closed path

The Range<Ty::Var> special case silently substituted Ty::I64 for an
unresolved inference variable before calling require_converted, which
meant Range<Ty::Var> would succeed where all other Ty::Var-containing
types correctly propagated an UnresolvedVar error to the caller.

Remove the coercion so require_converted(&args[0], ...) is called
directly; unresolved vars in Range now surface as UnresolvedVar and
abort compilation via the fail-closed check in enrich_program_ast().

Added test_ty_to_type_expr_range_with_var_is_unresolved_var to lock
this in. All 113 hew-serialize tests pass; workspace clippy -D warnings
clean.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: keep optional serializer omissions non-fatal

Required serializer conversions still fail closed, but best-effort inferred binding types and expr-type entries now continue as warnings/omissions instead of aborting compilation. Add a regression test covering unresolved local inferred types reaching the optional enrichment path.

* test(cli): rebase serializer regression on decl_span AST

commit.message:
fix(codegen): rebind actor sender structs after transfer (#559)

* fix(codegen): deep-copy owned fields in struct actor message params

Struct-typed receive fn parameters with owned fields (String, Vec,
HashMap) were not having those fields cloned at the actor boundary.
The sender's scope-exit drops could free the memory while the receiver
still held dangling references.

Three layered fixes:

1. MLIRGen emits a 'hew.struct_clone_fields' module attribute recording
   which struct fields need deep-copying (field index + clone function).
   deepCopyOwnedArgs in the lowering pass reads this metadata to
   extract, clone, and reinsert owned fields when packing struct
   message payloads.

2. Actor receive handler drop registration now uses
   includeStructTypes=true so struct params with owned fields get
   proper __auto_field_drop cleanup on scope exit.

3. ReceiveOpLowering now lowers handler param/result types through the
   type converter before creating LoadOp/AllocaOp/emitSizeOf, guarding
   against unlowered Hew dialect types reaching the LLVM verifier.

* fix(codegen): recurse into nested structs and transfer handles in deep-copy

Address two correctness gaps in the struct actor message deep-copy:

1. Nested structs with owned fields (e.g. Outer { inner: Inner } where
   Inner has a String field) were not recursively cloned.  The metadata
   emission now records nested owned-struct fields with a '__recurse'
   sentinel, and deepCopyOwnedArgs delegates to a new recursive helper
   (deepCopyStructFields) that follows those sentinels.

2. Handle-typed fields inside structs were not getting ownership
   transfer treatment.  Metadata now records them with
   '__handle_transfer', and after building the deep-copied struct,
   nullOutStructHandleFields zeros the handle slot in the sender's
   backing memory so the sender's drop becomes a no-op for transferred
   handles.  This prevents double-free when includeStructTypes=true
   enables receiver-side field drops.

* fix: exempt owned-field structs from sender unregisterDroppable

The three actor send paths (send, ask, arrow-send) check argument types
against String/Vec/HashMap/Closure to decide whether the sender retains
ownership after a message send.  Struct types were missing from this
exemption list.

With the deep-copy fix (commits 0c5c8ac, 160f488), owned fields inside
struct payloads are cloned at the actor boundary — the sender keeps its
originals.  But because structs fell through the exemption, the sender's
drop was removed via unregisterDroppable(), causing the original owned
fields (String, Vec, etc.) to leak.

Fix: add an LLVMStructType check using structHasOwnedFields() in all
three send paths.  Structs with owned fields keep their sender-side
drop.  Structs with only handle fields (structHasOwnedFields=false) are
correctly NOT exempted — handles are transferred with null-out, and the
sender need not drop.

* fix: support closure fields in struct actor messages

- Change metadata sentinel from "hew_rc_clone" to "__closure_clone" for
  closure-typed fields in hew.struct_clone_fields module attribute.
- Add __closure_clone branch in deepCopyStructFields: extract fn+env ptrs
  from the !llvm.struct<(ptr, ptr)> closure, clone env via hew_rc_clone,
  rebuild closure struct, and reinsert into parent struct.
- Add ClosureType ↔ LLVMStructType<(ptr,ptr)> coercion in both directions
  in coerceType, enabling closure values as struct field initializers and
  closure field reads from struct storage.
- Fix emitFieldDropsForUserStruct to extract env ptr from closure storage
  before calling hew_rc_drop (avoids unrealized_conversion_cast verifier
  failure on !llvm.struct<(ptr,ptr)> → !llvm.ptr).
- Add regression test: struct with closure field sent as actor message.

* fix(codegen): harden actor struct message ownership

* fix(codegen): restore actor receive user drops

* fix(codegen): rebind actor sender structs after transfer

* fix(codegen): transfer actor user-drop temps to receiver

Keep sender-side retention at the actor boundary limited to deep-copied values and auto-field-drop structs, so user-defined Drop structs transfer ownership cleanly even when passed as inline temporaries.

Stabilize the http_actor_handler regression around the repaired receiver-side drop timing by waiting on a ping barrier before printing each collected response.

* test(e2e): avoid moving http_actor_handler req.path

Use req.path directly in the handler so Drop still sees a valid field value after the send. This keeps the ping-barrier fixture shape but removes the move-before-Drop hazard behind the macOS http_actor_handler abort.

* fix(codegen): own single-part interpolated strings

Single-expression interpolations like f"{r}" were returning the\ninner string value directly. In for-await loops that let outer\nbindings alias the loop item and outlive its drop, which produced\ngarbage output and invalid frees in e2e_bytes_http_actor_handler on\nDarwin/arm64.\n\nRoute single-expression interpolations through concat-with-empty so\nthey allocate a fresh owned string before the original owner is\ndropped.\n\nCo-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
